channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: When AI cheats: The hidden dangers of reward hacking
item.description: New Anthropic research reveals how AI reward hacking leads to dangerous behaviors, including models giving harmful advice like drinking bleach to users seeking help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Forget cameras, AI, and chip upgrades – you really want better phone battery life
item.description: I asked TechRadar readers, "What's the most important phone feature?" The verdict came heavily down on battery life.
item.dc:creator: Roland Moore-Colyer, TechRadar

item.title: Think before you click: most free file-sharing apps expose your downloads to security risks, warns Surfshark
item.description: Most free file-sharing apps do not perform antivirus scans on files uploaded by their customers, warns Surfshark. Here's all you need to know.
item.dc:creator: Silvia Iacovcich, TechRadar

item.title: European Union slams Elon Musk’s X with a $140 million fine. Here’s why
item.description: European Union regulators on Friday fined Elon Musk’s social media platform X 120 million euros ($140 million) for breaches of the bloc’s digital regulations that they said could leave users exposed to scams and manipulation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Three steps to build a data foundation for federal AI innovation
item.description: Federal agencies are eager to accelerate innovation through AI, but most are held back by fragmented, unreliable and poorly governed data. The post Three steps to build a data foundation for federal AI innovation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Everyone wants to eat Nvidia’s lunch
item.description: So far, Nvidia has provided the vast majority of the processors used to train and operate large AI models like the ones that underpin ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: The VA’s size and complexity may be keeping top tech minds away, and veterans pay the price
item.description: "You begin with the end in mind and understanding the success metrics that are going to be important to drive this," said Sean O'Connor The post The VA’s size and complexity may be keeping top tech minds away, and veterans pay the price first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Cloudflare says it’s investigating the outage that brought down Zoom and LinkedIn
item.description: Internet infrastructure company Cloudflare on Friday said it was investigating an outage that took place in the morning that brought down several global websites including LinkedIn, Zoom and others, the second such crash to affect the company in less than three weeks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Chinese cyberspies target VMware vSphere for long-term persistence
item.description: Chinese state-sponsored threat actors are backdooring VMware vCenter and VMware ESXi servers with a malware program written in Go, allowing them to maintain long-term persistence in victim networks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New research reveals older users less likely to find AI useful
item.description: Cisco says we need to focus on giving everyone AI skills and opportunities, not just rolling it out quickly.
item.dc:creator: Craig Hale, TechRadar

item.title: Agents-as-a-service are poised to rewire the software industry and corporate structures
item.description: This was the year of AI agents. Chatbots that simply answered questions are now evolving into autonomous agents that can carry out tasks on a user’s behalf, so enterprises continue to invest in agentic platforms as transformation evolves.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google, Amazon, and xAI want to launch AI into space
item.description: Major tech firms are racing to deploy AI infrastructure in orbit, aiming to solve Earth’s digital bottlenecks and reshape how global systems connect, compute, and respond.
item.dc:creator: Eric Hal Schwartz, TechRadar

item.title: This tiny white tower packs an RTX 5060 GPU and a Ryzen 9 8945HX CPU, but looks nothing like a mini PC
item.description: Minisforum AtomMan G1 Pro mini PC pairs a mobile-class flagship CPU with a desktop GPU inside a narrow chassis, pushing cooling limits hard.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Bending Spoons continues its spree of buying famous tech brands with the Eventbrite deal
item.description: Bending Spoons buys Eventbrite for $500 million, aiming to revive growth using operational changes, pricing strategies, and product enhancements.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: CompTIA training targets workplace AI use
item.description: IT training and certification provider CompTIA this week released an updated version of its AI Essentials learning program designed to help employees develop AI skills for workplace use with tools such as ChatGPT, Copilot, and Gemini.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Figma wants to make working with AI more like working with humans
item.description: Over the past decade, Figma has transformed how people within companies collaborate to turn software ideas into polished products. Now the company is itself being transformed by AI.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Meta risks temporary ban on WhatsApp AI plan amid EU probe
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. risks a temporary European Union ban on the rollout of new policies over how its AI features in WhatsApp, after being hit by the latest probe into Big Tech's alleged dominance on the continent.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: US federal software reform bill aims to strengthen software management controls
item.description: Software management struggles that have pained enterprises for decades cause the same anguish to government agencies, and a bill making its way through the US House of Representatives to strengthen controls around government software management holds lessons for enterprises too.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: OpenAI to acquire AI training tracker Neptune
item.description: OpenAI has agreed to acquire a startup specializing in tools for tracking AI training, Neptune, which promptly announced it is withdrawing its products from the market. OpenAI said in a statement. The ChatGPT maker has been a Neptune customer for more than a year.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: There is no Dye in Apple’s design team
item.description: Steve Jobs said lots of things, so you’ll usually find a quote from him for almost every occasion. “Great things in business are never done by one person,” he once said. “They’re done by a team of people.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: India withdraws demand for forced installation of government app
item.description: The other day ComputerSweden reported that the Indian government had demanded that all mobile phones in the country be equipped with the government app Sanchar Saathi within 90 days.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why CIOs must reimagine ERP as the enterprise’s composable backbone
item.description: In my experience leading ERP modernization projects and collaborating with IT and business executives, I’ve learned that technology alone rarely determines success, but mindset and architecture do.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Developers urged to immediately upgrade React, Next.js
item.description: Developers using the React 19 library for building application interfaces are urged to immediately upgrade to the latest version because of a critical vulnerability that can be easily exploited by an attacker to remotely run their own code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Holiday travel privacy risks and how to stay safe
item.description: Travel companies share passenger data with third parties during holidays, but travelers can protect themselves by removing data from broker sites and using aliases.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Seven coding domains no developer really understands
item.description: We all want to be thought competent by our peers—to have them think we know what we are doing. And for the most part, we do, right? But come on, let’s be honest. There are a few things that just make our heads spin.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Police consider corporate manslaughter charges over UK Post Office software linked to 13 suicides
item.description: Police investigating the scandal around the UK Post Office Horizon IT system linked to the suicide of a numbers of its users are now considering corporate manslaughter charges against the companies involved, the National Police Chiefs’ Council (NPCC) has said.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Four things AWS needs to fix at re:Invent this week
item.description: The mood among Amazon Web Services customers is shifting from curiosity to urgency as the company prepares to once again to “re:Invent” itself at its annual customer conference this week.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Check if your passwords were stolen in huge leak
item.description: Threat intelligence firm Synthient uncovers one of the largest password exposures ever, prompting immediate security recommendations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
